note: Curated metadata for known-good GGUF-compatible models. HF IDs are verified at runtime — if a repo moves, the live search falls back gracefully. Download URLs are NEVER hardcoded here; always resolved live from the HF API.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder 1.5B Q8
models.description: The smallest Qwen Coder — runs on any hardware including CPU-only. Surprisingly capable for its size due to Qwen 2.5 distillation from larger models.
models.intended_use: Quick completions, simple fixes, code scaffolding, CPU/low-end GPU systems
models.tool_use: Basic function calling supported. JSON mode works. Reliability lower than 7B+ models — simple single-tool calls are fine.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder 3B Q8
models.description: Compact coder with strong performance per VRAM dollar. Trained on 5.5T code tokens — punches above its weight on completion tasks.
models.intended_use: Code completion, refactoring, debugging on 4–6 GB VRAM systems
models.tool_use: Function calling verified. Reliable for single-tool use; multi-step tool loops need clear prompting.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder 7B Q5
models.description: The sweet-spot Qwen Coder. Trained on 5.5T tokens across 92 languages. Beats many 14B models on HumanEval and MBPP benchmarks.
models.intended_use: Full-stack code generation, debugging, code review, shell automation, test writing
models.tool_use: Excellent — verified function calling, JSON mode, multi-turn tool loops. TheOrc's default worker model.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder 7B Q8
models.description: Full-precision Q8 variant of Qwen 2.5 Coder 7B. Near-lossless quality at the cost of extra VRAM — choose this when you have 10+ GB to spare.
models.intended_use: High-quality code generation where output accuracy matters more than VRAM budget
models.tool_use: Excellent — same as Q5 variant but with slightly higher accuracy on edge-case tool calls.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder 14B Q4
models.description: The 14B flagship Qwen Coder. Handles complex multi-file refactors, large context reasoning, and architecture-level decisions that smaller models miss.
models.intended_use: Complex architecture tasks, large codebase analysis, multi-file refactoring, senior-level code review
models.tool_use: Outstanding — best-in-class tool use reliability in this size range. Handles parallel tool calls and recursive tool loops reliably.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder 32B Q3
models.description: The 32B Qwen Coder — one of the strongest local code models available. Q3 quant makes it fit in 16-20 GB VRAM with minor quality trade-off.
models.intended_use: Enterprise-grade code generation, full-project scaffolding, deeply complex debugging sessions
models.tool_use: Elite — approaches GPT-4 level on structured tool use benchmarks. Ideal for TheOrc boss role on high-VRAM systems.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder 32B Q4
models.description: Full Q4 quality 32B Qwen Coder. For users with 24+ GB VRAM — the highest-quality local code model in TheOrc's tested catalog.
models.intended_use: Maximum quality local code generation — production-grade output on first attempt
models.tool_use: Elite — top-tier function calling, zero prompt engineering needed for tool use.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 7B Instruct
models.description: General-purpose Qwen 2.5 — strong at reasoning, instruction following, and mixed tasks. More versatile than the Coder variant for Boss/Orchestrator role.
models.intended_use: Task planning, decision making, reasoning about code architecture, mixed language tasks
models.tool_use: Verified function calling and JSON mode. Good for orchestration tasks requiring broad reasoning.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 14B Instruct
models.description: Large general-purpose Qwen 2.5 — excellent reasoning, planning, and multi-step instruction following. Strong Boss model candidate.
models.intended_use: Complex task decomposition, cross-domain reasoning, orchestrating multi-step pipelines
models.tool_use: Excellent multi-step tool use. Reliable at synthesising results from multiple tool calls into coherent plans.

models.name: Phi-4 Mini Q8
models.description: Microsoft's compact Phi-4 Mini — remarkable reasoning for 3.8B parameters. Trained with high-quality synthetic data emphasising logical chain-of-thought.
models.intended_use: Fast orchestration, task planning, quick reasoning tasks, Researcher role on VRAM-constrained systems
models.tool_use: Verified function calling. Punches well above weight on structured output — ideal for Boss role on 8 GB VRAM.

models.name: Phi-4 14B Q4
models.description: The full Phi-4 model — Microsoft's flagship small language model. Exceptional reasoning and math; trained on carefully curated synthetic data and textbooks.
models.intended_use: Complex reasoning, planning, multi-step problem solving — excellent Boss model for 12–16 GB VRAM setups
models.tool_use: Strong function calling support. Reliable JSON schema adherence. Recommended for Boss role when 16 GB VRAM available.

models.name: Phi-3.5 Mini Instruct
models.description: Microsoft's Phi-3.5 Mini — 128K context window in a 3.8B model. Strong at long document reasoning. Good for Researcher role.
models.intended_use: Long context reading, document summarisation, research synthesis, lightweight planning tasks
models.tool_use: Function calling supported. Works best on well-structured prompts.

models.name: Llama 3.1 8B Instruct
models.description: Meta's Llama 3.1 8B — the most downloaded open model of 2024. Excellent instruction following, strong on general tasks and coding. Very reliable.
models.intended_use: General coding, instruction following, summarisation, reasoning, multi-purpose Boss or Researcher
models.tool_use: Native function calling via Llama 3 tool-use format. One of the most battle-tested tool-use models in the open ecosystem.

models.name: Llama 3.2 3B Instruct
models.description: Meta's compact Llama 3.2 3B — designed for edge deployment. Fast, instruction-tuned, good for Researcher role where speed matters more than depth.
models.intended_use: Quick lookups, summarisation, lightweight research, fast responses on low-VRAM setups
models.tool_use: Function calling supported via Llama 3.2 format. Works well for simple single-tool calls.

models.name: Llama 3.3 70B Instruct
models.description: Meta's flagship 70B — delivers near-GPT-4 quality locally. Exceptional reasoning, coding, and instruction following. Requires 24+ GB VRAM.
models.intended_use: High-quality Boss model for large VRAM systems, complex architectural decisions, ambiguous multi-step tasks
models.tool_use: Elite function calling — one of the best open models for reliable multi-tool orchestration.

models.name: Mistral 7B Instruct v0.3
models.description: The original Mistral 7B — still competitive. Added function calling in v0.3. The model that proved 7B could match 13B models from earlier generations.
models.intended_use: General instruction following, code tasks, reliable baseline for any swarm role
models.tool_use: Function calling added in v0.3. Works with standard OpenAI-format tool schemas. Reliable for simple tool use.

models.name: Mistral Nemo 12B
models.description: Mistral Nemo — co-developed with NVIDIA. 128K context, strong multilingual capability. Excellent for research-heavy tasks requiring long document analysis.
models.intended_use: Long-context research, multilingual code projects, complex document understanding
models.tool_use: Function calling supported. Strong at structured output generation with long contexts.

models.name: DeepSeek Coder V2 Lite Q5
models.description: DeepSeek Coder V2 Lite uses Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) — 15.7B total params but only 2.4B active per token. Fast inference, strong code generation.
models.intended_use: Code generation, especially C/C++, Rust, systems programming. Fast inference via MoE architecture.
models.tool_use: Function calling supported. Strong on algorithmic and systems-level code tasks.

models.name: DeepSeek R1 Distill 7B (Qwen)
models.description: Distilled from DeepSeek-R1's chain-of-thought reasoning into a 7B Qwen backbone. Exhibits visible step-by-step reasoning (thinks before answering) — excellent Boss model.
models.intended_use: Task decomposition, multi-step planning, debugging complex logic, Boss/Orchestrator role
models.tool_use: Function calling works — slightly verbose due to CoT, but reliable. Produces well-reasoned tool use decisions.

models.name: DeepSeek R1 Distill 14B (Qwen)
models.description: Larger DeepSeek R1 distillation — 14B Qwen backbone with full chain-of-thought reasoning. Strong Boss candidate for 16 GB VRAM setups.
models.intended_use: Complex architectural planning, multi-agent orchestration, deep reasoning tasks
models.tool_use: Reliable function calling with explicit reasoning traces. Excellent for orchestrating multi-step swarm tasks.

models.name: Hermes 4 14B Q5
models.description: Nous Research's Hermes 4 — purpose-trained for tool use, agentic tasks, and structured output. One of the most reliable models for complex multi-step tool orchestration.
models.intended_use: Agentic workflows, complex tool orchestration, structured JSON generation, security research tasks
models.tool_use: Elite — specifically trained on tool-use datasets. Best-in-class for complex multi-tool scenarios. Recommended for security/pentest TheOrc profiles.

models.name: Hermes 3 Llama 3.1 8B
models.description: Hermes 3 fine-tuned on Llama 3.1 8B base — strong tool use and instruction following. Good Boss candidate for mid-range VRAM systems.
models.intended_use: Agentic tasks, tool use, complex instruction following, Boss role on 8–10 GB VRAM
models.tool_use: Excellent tool use — Nous Research's hallmark. Very reliable on TheOrc's tool schema.

models.name: Gemma 3 12B IT Q4
models.description: Google's Gemma 3 12B instruction-tuned. Strong multilingual capability, clean instruction following, good at document understanding. Popular choice for NVIDIA 8 GB systems.
models.intended_use: Code generation, document analysis, multilingual tasks, general assistance
models.tool_use: Function calling available but less battle-tested than Qwen/Llama for agentic use. Works for simple tool calls.

models.name: Gemma 2 9B IT
models.description: Gemma 2 9B instruction-tuned — Google's well-rounded mid-size model. Strong creative writing, summarisation, and instruction following. 8K context is the main limitation.
models.intended_use: General assistance, summarisation, code explanation, research tasks
models.tool_use: Limited — Gemma 2 has less reliable structured tool use than Llama/Qwen variants. Use for non-tool Researcher tasks.

models.name: Nemotron Mini 4B Q5
models.description: NVIDIA's Nemotron Mini — optimised for NVIDIA GPUs. Excellent on-device coding assistant with strong tool use. Part of NVIDIA's NGC ecosystem.
models.intended_use: Fast code completion, tool use on NVIDIA hardware, Worker role optimised for NVIDIA GPU throughput
models.tool_use: Verified function calling — purpose-built for agentic use on NVIDIA hardware. TheOrc's default NVIDIA worker model.

models.name: StarCoder2 3B
models.description: StarCoder2 3B — trained on 4T tokens of code from 619 programming languages. Best-in-class pure code completion for its size. Not instruction-tuned.
models.intended_use: Raw code completion (FIM), boilerplate generation, code translation — not for conversation or tool use
models.tool_use: None — not instruction-tuned. Pure fill-in-the-middle code model. Use as a fast completion engine, not an agent.

models.name: StarCoder2 15B
models.description: StarCoder2 15B — the flagship BigCode model. Exceptional raw code quality across 619 languages. Not chat-tuned; pairs well with a planning model.
models.intended_use: High-quality pure code generation, code search, repository-level completion
models.tool_use: None — use alongside an instruction-tuned boss model. Ideal as a dedicated code-generation worker.

models.name: Codestral 22B
models.description: Mistral AI's code-specialist model — 80+ languages, strong FIM (fill-in-the-middle). Designed explicitly for IDE integration. One of the best pure code models available locally.
models.intended_use: IDE code completion (FIM), code generation across 80+ languages, detailed code explanation
models.tool_use: Function calling supported. Reliable on structured output. Good Worker for code-heavy projects.
